refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR is already focused on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation latency is compatible with safe remote operation. It does not combine multiple requirements or include implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that the latency meets the safety compatibility criteria, such as p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation.

candidate_dps: Implement a dedicated low-latency communication protocol stack optimized for teleoperation
Design a real-time data compression and prioritization mechanism to minimize transmission delays
Establish a distributed control architecture with local autonomy to reduce dependency on end-to-end latency
Incorporate predictive control algorithms to compensate for communication delays
Use a modular communication interface that can adapt to varying network conditions
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement a dedicated low-latency communication protocol stack optimized for teleoperation

potential_sources: Tight integration between communication protocol and control system could cause coupling if not modularized
coupling_check.notes: Selecting a dedicated communication protocol as DP keeps the solution focused on latency requirements and allows modular implementation. Other DPs like predictive control or distributed control are complementary but add complexity and potential coupling.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency targets
Protocol stack can be implemented without excessive overhead
Operational policies do not impose constraints that increase latency beyond acceptable limits
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including adaptive mechanisms in the DP to handle variable network conditions and ensure latency targets are consistently met.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for enabling remote control and communication with the vehicle, including communication protocol handling for teleoperation

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional data flow for vehicle status and control feedback

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands and control instructions from remote operator

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network interface for communication

module_containment_scores.notes: Very broad module; contains many unrelated functions, leading to high diffusion of the DP across unrelated components.
Vehicle module focuses on physical vehicle systems; communication protocol for teleoperation is only partially contained and would diffuse into other vehicle subsystems.
Highly cohesive for the DP; communication protocol stack is central to teleoperation functionality with minimal diffusion outside this module.
Safety Services focus on safety alerts and monitoring; communication protocol stack is not well contained here and would cause significant diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Teleoperation module is the most specific and cohesive boundary for implementing the dedicated low-latency communication protocol stack optimized for teleoperation, as it directly manages remote control communication and related data flows.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Network Link interface responsibilities if physical network handling is not clearly separated.
If teleoperation communication protocols require integration with vehicle subsystems, some diffusion may occur across Vehicle module boundaries.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying a dedicated low-latency communication protocol stack optimized for teleoperation, which is necessary to achieve the required end-to-end latency compatible with safe remote operation (p95 ≤ 150 ms). This mapping is clear and focused on the functional requirement.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Network Link interface responsibilities if physical network handling is not clearly separated.
If teleoperation communication protocols require integration with vehicle subsystems, some diffusion may occur across Vehicle module boundaries.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Teleoperation module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2) for the DP, indicating strong cohesion and minimal diffusion risk. Other modules like NGHE Solution and Vehicle have high diffusion scores, making them less suitable. The Teleoperation module is the best architectural boundary for implementing the DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by testing the Teleoperation module's communication latency under agreed network conditions to ensure p95 ≤ 150 ms latency.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The DP is clearly allocated to the Teleoperation module, which owns the communication protocol stack and related interfaces, providing clear ownership and responsibility for implementation and verification.

recommended_revisions: Clarify and document the separation of responsibilities between the Teleoperation module and the Network Link interface to minimize overlap and diffusion risk.
Define clear integration boundaries and interfaces between the Teleoperation module and Vehicle subsystems to manage any necessary protocol interactions and reduce diffusion.
Consider adding explicit interface contracts or protocols to ensure that physical network handling and teleoperation communication logic remain well separated within their respective modules.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear, focused mappings and strong containment of the DP within the Teleoperation module. The DP directly addresses the FR with a dedicated communication protocol stack optimized for latency, and the IM allocation is clear with feasible verification. Although some diffusion risks exist, they are manageable through recommended revisions to clarify module boundaries and interfaces. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to stop decomposition here and focus on implementing and verifying the current design with the suggested clarifications rather than further decomposing the FR.

revise.why: To clarify and document the separation of responsibilities between the Teleoperation module and Network Link interface, and to define clear integration boundaries with Vehicle subsystems to minimize diffusion risk and ensure strong architectural containment.

assumptions: The Teleoperation module can implement the DP with sufficient performance to meet latency requirements.
Recommended revisions to module boundaries and interfaces will be implemented to mitigate diffusion risks.
open_risks: Potential overlap with Network Link interface responsibilities if separation is not clearly maintained.
Possible diffusion across Vehicle module boundaries if integration is not well defined.
evidence_needed: Verification test results demonstrating that the Teleoperation module meets the p95 ≤ 150 ms latency requirement under agreed network conditions.
Documentation of interface contracts and module boundary definitions to confirm separation and containment.
